Sprint Goal: Deliver a reliable local image optimization workflow from upload to processed download.
Planned Scope From Project Plan: Project setup; FastAPI API baseline; Swagger/OpenAPI documentation; Next.js dashboard shell; image and ZIP upload; validation; compression; format conversion; filename cleanup; processed image ZIP export; Sprint 1 demo notes.
Completed In Current Code: Local dev setup is in place. Backend has FastAPI app, health check, CORS, settings, storage folders, typed response models, Swagger UI, ReDoc, and OpenAPI export. Frontend has dashboard shell, app navigation, API client, TanStack Query provider, Image Optimizer page, upload UI, progress/error states, compression controls, output format selector, filename overrides, result table, processed image download, and ZIP export. Backend supports JPG/PNG/WebP uploads, ZIP extraction, fake image rejection, unsafe ZIP path rejection, Pillow compression, conversion, metadata stripping, safe filename cleanup, and processed file export.
Currently In Progress / Partially Done: Docker support was added and README ports were corrected. Sprint 1 checklist and progress CSV were added for submission evidence.
Pending / Carryover: No major Sprint 1 implementation gap remains. Optional submission items: attach screenshots or a short screen recording, and include test command output in the LMS submission if required.
Rubric Evidence - Goal Achievement & Qua: Sprint 1 goals are met for a working POC: users can upload images/ZIP files, process them, verify cleaned filenames and size reductions, and download optimized outputs.
Rubric Evidence - Process Documentation: Project plan, sprint milestone plan, capstone proposal, README setup instructions, issue template, Sprint 1 checklist, and this CSV are present in the repository. Recent commits show incremental work rather than one end-of-sprint dump.
Rubric Evidence - Technical Complexity &: Work demonstrates full-stack implementation: multipart upload handling, secure ZIP extraction, image validation, Pillow processing, format conversion, file persistence, typed OpenAPI contracts, and frontend upload/progress/result workflows.
Recommended Demo Focus: Demo Image Optimizer end-to-end: start app, open dashboard, upload image and ZIP, show validation, process to WebP/JPG, show filename cleanup and size reduction, download one image, download ZIP, then show Swagger docs and test evidence.

Sprint Goal: Add brand-aware AI image metadata generation and review/export workflows.
Planned Scope From Project Plan: Ollama image metadata service; dual-model vision/language flow; brand context document upload; brand context UI; SEO metadata page; metadata generation; regenerate one image; SEO filename download; CSV export.
Completed In Current Code: Backend has AI metadata service with preview image generation, Ollama client integration, JSON extraction/validation, invalid JSON retry, failed-row handling, brand context injection, and dual vision/language model support. Backend accepts TXT/DOCX/PDF brand documents, extracts text, truncates context, and stores it with the image job. Frontend has SEO Metadata page that loads an image job, uploads brand documents, previews extracted context, generates metadata, shows model settings, supports per-image regenerate, supports local field edits, opens image previews/downloads with SEO filenames, and exports selected CSV fields.
Currently In Progress / Partially Done: Local metadata edits exist in the UI, but final persisted approvals are not yet fully implemented. Live AI quality depends on having Ollama and the selected local models installed.
Pending / Carryover: Persist metadata edits to the backend; add accept/approve actions; add bulk approval workflow; polish table-level review states for final review workflow.
Rubric Evidence - Goal Achievement & Qua: Most Sprint 2 functional goals are implemented and usable after an image job exists. Brand context and CSV export are working. The main remaining gap is persisted approval/review state.
Rubric Evidence - Process Documentation: The README documents optional Ollama setup. Backend tests cover AI metadata behavior with fake clients, retry handling, brand context prompt inclusion, CSV export, and failure states. Sprint status is summarized in this CSV.
Rubric Evidence - Technical Complexity &: Work demonstrates AI integration, prompt control, JSON validation/retry, document extraction, local file persistence, frontend review UI, CSV export selection, and typed API usage.
Recommended Demo Focus: Demo after Sprint 1 image upload: open SEO Metadata, load job ID, upload brand TXT/DOCX/PDF, show extracted context, generate metadata, regenerate one row, preview/download image with SEO filename, export metadata CSV.

Sprint Goal: Build crop review and first website quality workflow foundations.
Planned Scope From Project Plan: Natural-language resize instruction parser; exact crop and fit-inside processing; AI crop suggestion endpoint; Image Resizer UI; persisted review approvals; website crawler skeleton; broken-link checker skeleton; export/report hardening.
Completed In Current Code: Backend has resize instruction parsing, exact crop mode, fit-inside padding mode, crop focus settings, AI crop suggestion endpoint, crop review endpoint, and crop-aware image processing. Frontend has Image Resizer page with upload flow, presets, custom dimensions, instruction parsing, AI crop request, crop review modal, manual crop fallback, resize result table, processed image preview/download, and ZIP export. Website quality routes exist for pages, links, and broken links, but they currently return placeholder empty lists.
Currently In Progress / Partially Done: Crop/resize foundation is active and partially implemented. Website quality workflow is only scaffolded. Job history/queueing and broad job management are not implemented; current POC uses synchronous endpoints and local JSON/file storage.
Pending / Carryover: Persist approved crop decisions; persist metadata/crop approvals together; implement website job creation; implement same-domain crawler; implement URL status checks and broken-link source reporting; add CSV/JSON/XLSX website report exports; add final Sprint 3 demo notes.
Rubric Evidence - Goal Achievement & Qua: Crop review work shows visible progress beyond Sprint 1 and Sprint 2, but Sprint 3 is not complete yet. Website checker and broken-link checking still need real functionality before they can be demoed as completed.
Rubric Evidence - Process Documentation: Sprint 3 status is clearly documented as partial. Existing project plan and sprint milestone plan describe remaining work and carryover. Current code separates finished image workflows from pending website workflows.
Rubric Evidence - Technical Complexity &: Work already includes non-trivial crop math, exact resizing, fit-inside canvas behavior, AI subject crop suggestion parsing, normalized crop boxes, and frontend crop review controls. Remaining website crawler/link checking will add network crawling and reporting complexity.
Recommended Demo Focus: Demo Image Resizer as work in progress: upload images, choose preset/custom size, parse instruction like '600 x 400 WebP', request AI crop when Ollama is available, review crop suggestions/manual fallback, process resized output, download ZIP. Clearly state website quality tools are scaffolded but pending.
